source.title: Chemical Reaction and Release in Atchison, Kansas - MGPI Processing, Inc.

event.top_event: Inadvertent mixing of sulfuric acid and sodium hypochlorite resulting in chlorine gas release
event.incident_type: Chemical reaction and toxic gas release
actions_taken: Emergency responders closed discharge valve on cargo tank
Mod B deluge (sprinkler) system manually activated
Water spray deployed to suppress plume
Atchison County Department of Emergency Management issued shelter-in-place order for 11,000 citizens
Evacuation orders issued to schools and residents north of facility
800 middle and high school students transferred upwind to south side of Atchison
Air quality monitoring conducted by EPA and environmental consultants
Three MGPI operators evacuated building and sought medical attention
event.summary: On October 21, 2016, at approximately 7:35 AM, a Harcros Chemicals cargo tank motor vehicle arrived at MGPI Processing facility in Atchison, Kansas to deliver 30% sulfuric acid. During unloading operations, the driver inadvertently connected the sulfuric acid discharge hose to the unsecured sodium hypochlorite fill line instead of the sulfuric acid fill line. Approximately 4,000 gallons of sulfuric acid mixed with 5,850 gallons of sodium hypochlorite in the bulk tank, initiating a highly exothermic reaction that produced a greenish-yellow toxic gas cloud containing chlorine and other compounds. The cloud migrated northeast from the facility, impacting workers onsite and community members. Over 140 individuals sought medical attention; one MGPI employee and five community members were hospitalized. The incident was mitigated by emergency responders closing the cargo tank discharge valve and activating the facility's deluge system.
recommendations: Design chemical transfer equipment with physical separation between incompatible fill lines
Implement uniquely shaped and color-coded hose couplings for each chemical or class of chemicals
Install automated or remote emergency shut-off systems for chemical unloading operations
Improve pipe markings and labeling to clearly identify chemical lines
Enhance chemical unloading procedures to include verification steps and secondary confirmation
Ensure emergency escape respirator face pieces are stored in readily accessible locations
Conduct comprehensive process hazard analysis (PHA) considering human factors
Implement management of change procedures for equipment modifications
Provide training to drivers and operators on chemical incompatibilities and safe unloading practices
Establish communication protocols between facility operators and delivery drivers

hazards.name: Chemical unloading operations involving hazardous materials
Toxic gas generation from chemical reactions

threats.name: Incorrect fill line connection during unloading
Unsecured or accessible incompatible fill lines
Inadequate physical separation of incompatible chemical lines
Identical fill line connections and locks
Poor communication between operator and driver regarding fill line location
Missing or damaged dust cap securing mechanisms

consequences.name: Onsite worker exposure to toxic gas
Community exposure to toxic gas plume
Hospitalization of workers and community members

controls.name: Cam lever dust caps on fill lines
controls.barrier_role: Prevent unauthorized access to fill lines and protect connection points
controls.lod_basis: Physical barrier to prevent incorrect connections

supporting_text: Cam lever dust caps prevent access to fill lines by capping the end of the fill lines when they are not in use
The sodium hypochlorite dust cap was unable to be locked because it was missing a split ring on one lever
Without the split ring on one of the cam levers, it was impossible to secure the dust cap on the fill line with the padlock

controls.name: Padlock and split rings securing dust caps
controls.barrier_role: Prevent removal of dust caps and unauthorized access to fill lines
controls.lod_basis: Physical locking mechanism to secure dust caps

supporting_text: Cam lever dust caps are secured by engaging two levers and locking them in place with a padlock and two split rings
The sodium hypochlorite dust cap was missing a split ring on one lever
The CSB was unable to determine why the sodium hypochlorite dust cap was missing a split ring nor how long it was left unsecured

controls.name: Physical separation of incompatible fill lines
controls.barrier_role: Reduce likelihood of incorrect connection by increasing distance between incompatible chemical lines
controls.lod_basis: Passive control using distance to prevent mixing

supporting_text: The five chemical fill lines in the Mod B chemical transfer area were all located near each other; significantly, the sodium hypochlorite fill line was about 18 inches from the sulfuric acid fill line
The CSB determined that the chemical fill lines were first installed with this same configuration at Mod B in 1996
Physically isolating or using distance to separate fill lines can lower the risk of incorrect connections

controls.name: Uniquely shaped and color-coded hose couplings
controls.barrier_role: Prevent incorrect hose connections through incompatible coupling designs

supporting_text: Since both fill lines had the same diameter and orientation, the driver could connect the sulfuric acid hose to the incorrect, but unlocked, sodium hypochlorite fill line
Work with motor carriers to select hose couplings and fill line connections with uniquely shaped and color-coded fittings for each chemical or class of chemicals

controls.name: Operator identification and communication of correct fill line
controls.barrier_role: Ensure driver connects to correct fill line through verbal communication and pointing
controls.lod_basis: Human-based verification of correct fill line location

supporting_text: The operator reports that he pointed out the location of the sulfuric acid fill line to the driver and that the driver acknowledged the location; the driver, however, reports that the operator did not point out the fill line
The operator then returned to the Mod B building at approximately 7:47 AM before he saw the driver connect the discharge hose to the fill line
The driver removed the dust cap from the first unlocked fill line that he saw at the facility, which he assumed to be the sulfuric acid fill line

controls.name: Operator presence during hose connection
controls.barrier_role: Verify correct fill line connection and prevent incorrect connections through direct observation
controls.lod_basis: Direct supervision and observation of connection process

supporting_text: The operator then returned to the Mod B building at approximately 7:47 AM before he saw the driver connect the discharge hose to the fill line
It was, and is, MGPI's practice to unlock fill line dust caps only for delivery

controls.name: Emergency escape respirator face pieces
controls.barrier_role: Protect workers from toxic gas exposure during emergency evacuation
controls.lod_basis: Personal protective equipment for emergency response

supporting_text: They immediately attempted to access their emergency escape respirator face pieces, but were unable to do so because the respirator face pieces were not stored in a readily accessible location or had been moved
The night and day shift operators attempted to retrieve their respirator face pieces from their respective lockers but were unable to do so

The trainee did not have a locker because MGPI does not assign lockers to trainees. The trainee normally kept his respirator on a counter in the control room; however, the previous shift moved it because they needed counter space

controls.name: Deluge (sprinkler) system activation
controls.barrier_role: Suppress and mitigate toxic gas plume through water spray
controls.lod_basis: Automated and manual emergency mitigation system

supporting_text: MGPI worked with the fire department to develop a plan to mitigate the release

Responders were able to safely close the discharge valve on the cargo tank and turn off the truck engine. They also set up a water spray and manually activated the Mod B deluge (sprinkler) system to suppress the plume generated by the chemical reaction

controls.name: Emergency response procedures and coordination
controls.barrier_role: Coordinate emergency response to minimize impact of toxic gas release

supporting_text: The Atchison County Department of Emergency Management (ACDEM) ordered thousands of community members to shelter-in-place and other community members to evacuate in some areas
ACDEM issued evacuation orders to schools and residents to the north who were not in the plume. Buses safely transferred 800 middle and high school students upwind to the south side of Atchison
By approximately 10:30 AM, the Kansas Highway Patrol reported to emergency responders that the plume was on the north and west outskirts of the city and dissipating rapidly

controls.name: Process hazard analysis (PHA)
controls.barrier_role: Identify and assess chemical incompatibilities and hazards in unloading operations
controls.lod_basis: Systematic hazard identification and risk assessment

supporting_text: facility management must pay careful attention to the design and operation of chemical transfer equipment by applying the hierarchy of controls and by considering human factors to reduce the opportunity for inadvertent mixing
Conduct comprehensive process hazard analysis (PHA) considering human factors

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
